claims.claim: The Malaysian Hindu Sangam documented 17 Hindu temple demolitions in Selangor between 2020 and 2025.
claims.source: No primary source found. Related but non-confirming source: Bernama, 2026-03-08, reports MHS working with authorities and cases involving two temples in Hulu Selangor, but does not state 17 demolitions: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2531872
claims.correction: Do not state the 17-demolition count unless the MHS document or an official demolition register is produced. Current wording should be changed to 'The analysis could not verify a published MHS count of 17 demolitions.'
claims.stale_check: No stale-change issue found for 2020-2025; later 2026 data concerns unauthorised temples, not demolitions.

claims.claim: 17 Temple Demolitions in Selangor Since 2020 Despite Court Orders
claims.source: No primary source found for 17 Selangor demolitions since 2020 or for court orders covering those cases. Related Selangor State Assembly data addresses unauthorised temples, not demolitions: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/
claims.correction: Headline should be withdrawn or heavily qualified until a case list with court-order references is available.
claims.stale_check: No evidence that a post-publication change made this accurate.

claims.claim: including 4 where court injunctions were in force at the time of demolition
claims.source: No primary court orders or official contempt/demolition records found for four Selangor cases in 2020-2025. Historical non-primary reports discuss older 2006-2007 injunction incidents but not this claim: https://www.necf.org.my/newsmaster.cfm?action=view&menuid=154&retrieveid=998
claims.correction: Replace with a verified list of the four temples, injunction dates, issuing courts, case numbers, and demolition dates, or remove.
claims.stale_check: The available injunction examples found are older than the claimed 2020-2025 period.

claims.claim: 4 temples were demolished while court injunctions were active.
claims.source: No primary court source found. Search found 2026 Rawang reporting of a halt order/demolition dispute, outside the 2020-2025 claim, but no primary court document was available.
claims.correction: This is a high-risk legal claim and should not be published without the court documents and case chronology.
claims.stale_check: A 2026 Rawang dispute may be a later development but does not verify the 2020-2025 statement.

claims.claim: Local authorities proceeded despite court orders.
claims.source: No primary court orders or enforcement records found for the alleged 2020-2025 Selangor cases.
claims.correction: Identify the specific local authorities, the orders breached, and the legal status of each order, or remove the claim.

claims.source: No primary court docket search result or official court registry record found. Historical secondary source reports contempt proceedings in older temple-demolition matters, showing this type of claim requires docket-level verification: https://www.necf.org.my/newsmaster.cfm?action=view&menuid=154&retrieveid=998
claims.correction: Do not assert absence of contempt proceedings without checking the relevant court dockets for each case.

claims.claim: An estimated 3,200 Hindu temples in Malaysia lack proper land title.
claims.source: No primary national register found for 3,200 untitled Hindu temples. Related 2025 Selangor Assembly answer recorded 517 unlicensed Hindu temples in Selangor as of 2025-06-30: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/ . Later 2026 official/state reporting recorded 687 or 688 unauthorised Hindu temples in Selangor, but that is not a national land-title figure: https://berita.rtm.gov.my/nasional/senarai-berita-nasional/senarai-artikel/688-kuil-operasi-tanpa-kebenaran-di-selangor/
claims.correction: Replace with verified jurisdiction-specific data. For Selangor, use the official 517 figure as of 2025-06-30, or note later 2026 figures separately if updating the article.
claims.stale_check: There was a later change: Selangor official figures moved from 517 unlicensed temples as of 2025-06-30 to 687/688 unauthorised temples in 2026 reporting. This does not verify a nationwide 3,200 figure.

claims.claim: Most were built on estate land before independence.
claims.source: U.S. Department of State 2006 Malaysia human rights report says several NGOs complained that unregistered Hindu temples and shrines were often constructed on privately owned plantations prior to independence, later transferred to government ownership: https://www.refworld.org/reference/annualreport/usdos/2007/en/43240 . Peer-reviewed related source: Noriyuki Segawa, 'Temples Against Development: A Case Study of Hinduism in Malaysia', Religion and Society, 2019: https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/religionandsociety/25/0/25_17/_article/-char/en
claims.correction: Change 'Most' to 'Many contested or unregistered temples are reported to have originated on plantation or estate land before independence.' The source supports a pattern, not a majority count.
claims.stale_check: Historical claim; no stale-change issue identified.

claims.claim: When estates were developed, temples had no legal standing.
claims.source: The Selangor State Assembly's 2025 answer describes enforcement for unlicensed Hindu temples through illegal-occupation notices and relocation to gazetted non-Muslim worship sites, indicating lack of permission matters legally: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/ . However, no primary source found supporting the absolute phrase 'no legal standing' for all such temples.
claims.correction: Avoid the absolute. Say 'many lacked formal land title or permission and were vulnerable to enforcement under land law.'

claims.claim: The 2009 Cabinet Committee on Temple Relocation has relocated only 34 temples.
claims.source: No primary government record found confirming a 2009 Cabinet Committee on Temple Relocation or the figure of 34 relocations. Related but non-matching 2025 Bernama/Selangor Assembly reporting says Selangor LIMAS approved 400 land slots for non-Muslim houses of worship since 2008: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php/politics/general/news.php?id=2443552
claims.correction: Remove unless the committee's official terms, date of establishment, and relocation statistics are produced.
claims.stale_check: Later available data suggests a different institutional frame, LIMAS, and a broader 400 land-slot figure since 2008, but it does not directly refute a separate committee claim.

claims.claim: At the current rate, the backlog of 3,200 untitled temples will take 280 years.
claims.source: No primary source verifies either the 3,200 backlog or 34 relocations since 2009. Selangor State Assembly 2025 answer projects 10% resolution of 274 unauthorised temples on government land by 2025-12-31, not a 280-year national backlog: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/
claims.correction: Remove the 280-year calculation; it compounds two unverified inputs and ignores jurisdiction-specific official targets.
claims.stale_check: Later 2026 Selangor data changed the denominator for Selangor, further undermining the calculation.

claims.claim: Hindu Malaysians comprise 6.3% of the population.
claims.source: Department of Statistics Malaysia, Key Findings Population and Housing Census of Malaysia 2020, reports Hinduism at 6.1% in 2020: https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/publication-estatistik-log?chapter_id=2825&document_id=738 . U.S. State Department 2020 religious freedom report cites the 2010 census figure of 6.3% Hindu: https://www.ecoi.net/en/document/2051656.html
claims.correction: Use 6.1% for the latest census-based national figure. 6.3% is the 2010 census figure, not the current 2020 figure.
claims.stale_check: This appears stale: 6.3% was correct for the 2010 census but superseded by the 2020 census figure of 6.1%.

claims.claim: Temple demolitions disproportionately affect a minority community with limited political leverage.
claims.source: DOSM verifies Hindus are a minority nationally at 6.1% in Census 2020: https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/publication-estatistik-log?chapter_id=2825&document_id=738 . No primary source found measuring 'limited political leverage' or disproportionality of demolition impact.
claims.correction: Separate the verified demographic point from the analytical claim. State that Hindus are a small religious minority, while any claim about political leverage should be attributed to named experts or omitted.
claims.stale_check: No stale-change issue identified except the demographic percentage.

claims.claim: When court orders do not stop demolitions, minority rights exist on paper but not on the ground.
claims.source: No primary source verifies the premise that current Selangor demolitions occurred despite court orders. Federal Constitution Article 11 protects freedom of religion, but that does not verify the demolition premise: https://www.jac.gov.my/spk/images/stories/10_akta/perlembagaan_persekutuan/federal_constitution.pdf
claims.correction: This reframe rests on unverified court-order claims and should be removed or rewritten after legal verification.

claims.claim: Temple regularisation requires a dedicated land audit, fast-track titling process, and meaningful penalties for demolishing protected structures.
claims.source: This is a policy recommendation, not directly fact-checkable. Related official source: Selangor State Assembly 2025 answer says the state uses integrated governance and legal enforcement, with relocation to gazetted non-Muslim worship sites: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/
claims.correction: Frame explicitly as an editorial recommendation, not a factual finding.

claims.claim: The 2009 committee approach has demonstrably failed.
claims.source: No primary source found confirming the existence, remit, or outcome statistics of the alleged 2009 committee. Related Selangor LIMAS data show approved land slots since 2008 but do not establish failure: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php/politics/general/news.php?id=2443552
claims.correction: Remove or replace with a sourced evaluation of a named committee, its mandate, targets, outputs, and current backlog.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 17 Temple Demolitions in Selangor Since 2020 Despite Court Orders
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Specific count, location, period, and legal premise could not be traced to a primary source.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: No primary source found; related Selangor Assembly data is about unlicensed temples, not demolitions: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 4 temples were demolished while court injunctions were active.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The claim requires court orders, dates, case numbers, and demolition records; none were found.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Local authorities proceeded despite court orders. No contempt proceedings followed.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: This asserts unlawful conduct and absence of legal consequences without docket-level verification.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: No primary court docket source found.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: An estimated 3,200 Hindu temples in Malaysia lack proper land title.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary national register or official estimate was found for 3,200 untitled Hindu temples.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Selangor State Assembly instead records 517 unlicensed Hindu temples in Selangor as of 2025-06-30: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Most were built on estate land before independence.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Sources support that many or often some unregistered temples originated on plantation land, but not that most of the alleged 3,200 did.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: U.S. Department of State 2006 Malaysia report: https://www.refworld.org/reference/annualreport/usdos/2007/en/43240

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The 2009 Cabinet Committee on Temple Relocation has relocated only 34 temples.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary record found for the committee or the 34-temple output figure.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: No primary source found; related Bernama report on Selangor LIMAS says 400 land slots approved since 2008: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php/politics/general/news.php?id=2443552

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: At the current rate, the backlog of 3,200 untitled temples will take 280 years.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The calculation relies on unverified numerator and denominator and ignores newer jurisdiction-specific figures.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Hindu Malaysians comprise 6.3% of the population.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The latest official census figure located is 6.1% in 2020; 6.3% corresponds to older 2010 data.

omitted_facts: Selangor State Assembly answered that as of 2025-06-30 there were 517 unlicensed Hindu temples in Selangor, with 274 on government land and 243 on privately owned land: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/

The same Selangor Assembly answer said the state expected to resolve 10% of the 274 unauthorised temples on government land, or 27 temples, by 2025-12-31: https://dewan.selangor.gov.my/question/kuil-hindu-tidak-berlesen-dibina-di-selangor/

Later 2026 reporting from RTM/Bernama cites Selangor officials saying 688 Hindu temples operated without permission in Selangor as of April 2026, with 433 on government land and 255 on private land; this is a later figure and should not be back-applied to a 2025 claim without explanation: https://berita.rtm.gov.my/nasional/senarai-berita-nasional/senarai-artikel/688-kuil-operasi-tanpa-kebenaran-di-selangor/

Bernama reported on 2025-07-10 that Selangor's LIMAS committee had approved 400 land slots for non-Muslim houses of worship since 2008, a governance fact omitted from the analysis: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php/politics/general/news.php?id=2443552
DOSM Census 2020 reports Hindus at 6.1% of Malaysia's population, not 6.3%: https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/publication-estatistik-log?chapter_id=2825&document_id=738

Bernama reported on 2026-03-08 that MHS said it was working with about 35 religious bodies and authorities, had discussed specific cases in Kuala Kubu Bharu/Hulu Selangor, and had formed a legal team; this complicates a frame that only depicts institutional failure: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2531872
source_assessment: The original analysis lists no sources, so its source diversity is effectively zero. Its strongest claims require Malaysian Hindu Sangam records, Selangor land-office data, local-authority demolition records, and court files, but none are cited. The verification found relevant official or quasi-official material from Selangor State Assembly, Bernama/RTM, DOSM, and the U.S. State Department, plus one peer-reviewed article, but the original analysis itself does not represent government, temple committees, courts, minority-rights advocates, developers, or local-authority perspectives in a traceable way.
overall_assessment: The analysis is not publication-safe in its current form. The central headline claim, the 17-demolition count, the four active injunctions, the absence of contempt proceedings, the 3,200 untitled-temple estimate, the 34-relocation figure, and the 280-year backlog calculation are all unverified against primary sources. One concrete demographic claim is outdated or wrong: the latest DOSM census figure is 6.1% Hindu, while 6.3% is a 2010 figure. The issue is real and supported by official data showing many unauthorised Hindu temples in Selangor, but this analysis overstates legal and numerical claims without sourcing.
